findings.title: Reset tokens are generated with a non-cryptographic PRNG
Successful reset-token verification does not consume the token

reasoning: Password hashing uses bcrypt at cost 12 and bcrypt.compare. The reset-token digest comparison is length-checked and constant-time. The reset limiter is registered before the reset route, and the reset response is generic. However, reset tokens use Math.random rather than a CSPRNG, and verification only checks record.used without setting it, so a verified token remains reusable until another component changes the record.
